channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Spotify’s Daniel Ek is bringing his body-scanning clinics to the US
item.description: Spotify founder Daniel Ek's body-scanning startup, Neko Health, is setting its sights on the United States after raising $700 million from a star-studded group of celebrities, entrepreneurs, and...

item.title: Building Autonomous AI Agents on Solana — Why Execution Speed Changes Everything
item.description: Solana processes 400ms finality and handles thousands of transactions per second. Smart contracts on this chain are fast, deterministic, and predictable — which is exactly why they're limited....

item.title: GitHub Copilot Now Prevents Vulnerabilities Before Code Commits
item.description: GitHub just shipped the most useful security feature in the Copilot app since Code Scanning — and it is a slash command. No experimental-mode opt-in. No terminal incantation. No Copilot Business...

item.title: API Key Rotation Without Downtime: Patterns and Implementation
item.description: Rotating API keys is routine maintenance — until it causes a production incident. The window between revoking an old key and deploying a new one is where things break: services return 401s, alerts...

item.title: The Line Is Not Between Human and Machine... It Is Between Code and Judgment.
item.description: I have been writing a series with one claim at its center: you cannot be your own verifier. That still holds, but I had been using it too loosely. The actor and the auditor can be different and...

item.title: Stop Writing LinkedIn Posts by Hand - Here's the n8n Workflow I Built
item.description: If you're building automations with n8n, LinkedIn content is one of the more satisfying workflows to tackle because the manual version is genuinely painful at scale, and the automated version is...

item.title: Stop describing pixels to your AI agent — annotate them instead
item.description: Stop describing pixels to your AI agent — annotate them instead If you pair-program with an AI agent — Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, whatever — you've lived this moment. You take a screenshot of...

item.title: When AI Beats a Senior Developer — and When It Doesn't
item.description: Last week the agent wrote a 200-line CSV-to-JSON normalizer with edge cases I'd have spent forty minutes on, in about ninety seconds. The same afternoon, on a multi-tenant billing edge case, it...

item.title: Crypto OTC Proof-of-Funds PDF Fraud Detection
item.description: Originally published at htpbe.tech. The version on htpbe.tech stays in sync with the latest detection algorithm — refer to it for the canonical text. A self-introduced broker emails the desk on...

item.title: How I Built a Multilingual CEFR Language Testing Platform in PHP
item.description: When I started building Examinizer, the goal was simple: make CEFR language certification accessible online without expensive test centres. The Stack PHP 8.2 backend with MySQL 8.0 Static...

item.title: `new` Operator in Java
item.description: The new operator is one of the first Java keywords every developer learns. Every time you create an object, you're using new. But what actually happens behind the scenes? Where is the object...

item.title: My Ebike Delivery Went Missing. When I Tried to Recover It, I Ended Up in Chatbot Hell
item.description: Companies’ increasing reliance on AI chatbots isn’t making the customer service experience smarter. It’s just making it more infuriating.

item.title: The PS6 sure sounds like a handheld
item.description: The video game industry is in turmoil. Microsoft and Sony are starting to pivot to their next consoles, but it's not looking great: Prices are soaring, Sony is killing the video game disc, and...

item.title: Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
item.description: Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. The...

item.title: OpenAI Staffers Are Funding a Rival Super PAC to Take on Their Boss
item.description: OpenAI employees have donated more than $215,000 to a political effort opposing Leading the Future, a group backed by the company’s president, Greg Brockman.

item.title: Starlink’s V5 dish is now available — here’s how it compares
item.description: SpaceX's latest residential dish - the Starlink V5 - is now available in "select areas." It's notably smaller and lighter than the V4 dish with improved power efficiency. It'll be available in...

item.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
item.description: SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary...

item.title: Google and Epic give up fighting — third-party Android app stores are coming next week
item.description: Epic Games and Google have just jointly withdrawn their attempt to retroactively settle the lawsuit that's changing how Android app stores work in the United States - and that means Google will be...

item.title: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang in talks to launch AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B
item.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

item.title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
item.description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.

item.title: Nothing’s good-looking Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is just $69
item.description: While most fitness trackers are losing the screens to keep the price low, the CMF by Nothing Watch 3 Pro is a bit different. The budget-friendly smartwatch with a 1.43-inch OLED display is even...

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and...

item.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
item.description: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

item.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

item.title: Microsoft’s biggest ever patch Tuesday lets you hold off updating for longer
item.description: Microsoft just released a long list of improvements for Windows 11 as part of its bigger patch Tuesdays, patching at least 570 security holes, almost triple the number fixed in last month's...

item.title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
item.description: OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report from Bloomberg. The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
item.description: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its Security...

item.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
item.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to...

item.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
item.description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem...
